issues.title: Wrong message order for JDBC Chat Memory

comments.body: Yes, and I think the saveAll function should add @Transactional to ensure the data consistency
I pushed a PR https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/pull/3154 to fix this issues
This has now been fixed in the commit 31da8f3dbb63909bab52c9855ea854a553a3349d  thanks for all who reported.

> not resolved

I guess you're referring to the timestamp issue. In fact, as @markpollack  mentioned, the `timestamp` field is not actually a field that represents the "message sending time"—it's simply an auto-incrementing sequence. The name of this field is misleading, and it will be adjusted in the future. 

Additionally, what `ChatMemoryRepository` stores is not exactly "historical conversation messages"; it's more like a message window that ensures the total number of messages does not exceed a specified limit. When the number of messages exceeds the maximum size, older messages are evicted.

issues.title: default the dialect for JdbcChatMemoryRepository
issues.body:
if I dont specify a dialect, it complains I need to specify it. but of course the easiest way to get the dialect is from the DataSource, which I sin the `JdbcTemplate`, which you require that I specify anyway. Could the implementation default to the `DataSource` obtained from the `JdbcTemplate`? 



```
       var jdbc = JdbcChatMemoryRepository
                .builder()
                .dialect(JdbcChatMemoryRepositoryDialect.from(Objects.requireNonNull(sql.getDataSource())))
                .jdbcTemplate(sql)
                .build();
```

Also, could we redesign this to instead just require a `DataSource` (and the implementation can prepare its own `JdbcTemplate`?)

comments.body: This would seem to work.  I had issues when developing this wrt hsqldb, but that seemed to be pilot error.

issues.title: pgvector's initializer should use the same name as is used everywhere else (always, never, embedded)
